text.title: AI Code Lint Presets Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the lint-summary shape, the noqa-floor discipline, and the per-PR exemption rule.

section.title: Required lint-summary fields

list.item: preset_version (string; matches the version of the presets file checked in)
counts_by_category (object: no_op_tests, broad_excepts, magic_literals, debug_statements, fabricated_imports, other)

section.reference: PR comment template indexed by category.

list.item: Pre-existing violations are tagged with explicit `# noqa: RULE-CODE # baseline` on adoption via `scripts/adopt.sh`. NEVER blanket-disable a rule.
Every noqa MUST include a reason comment after the `#`. Bare `# noqa` is FORBIDDEN by a secondary check.
Baseline noqa count MUST trend down over time; CI emits a `noqa_count` metric and a target reduction rate.
Exemptions in new code (`# noqa` added in the PR) MUST come with a `because: ` annotation.
More than 3 new noqa lines in a single PR triggers an `escalate` review tag.
Per-rule disablement at file or repo level requires a CODEOWNERS sign-off; AI assistants are FORBIDDEN from editing the presets.

antipattern.description: Lint passes because the AI added `# noqa` to silence the rule it failed.
antipattern.reason: r1 violation. Rule downgrading via noqa is the dominant escape route. CI must detect added-noqa-by-AI commits.
Defeats the baseline rule; future maintainers cannot evaluate the exemption.

antipattern.description: `counts_by_category` empty when scope is `full-tree` on a non-trivial repo.
antipattern.reason: Suggests lint did not run or output was discarded.

list.item: noqa_floor.justified is `true` for the adoption commit.
Every category count is >= 0 (zeros are recorded, not omitted).
Recommendation matches the rule: any nonzero category → `revise`.
